metadata.name: gromologist
metadata.summary: Library to handle various GROMACS-related stuff

methods.name: gromologist.Parser.SelectionParser._select_set_atoms
documentation:
Main recursive fn taking care of stratifying the input
\:param selection_string: str, selection string
\:return: set of int, output atom indices

classes.name: gromologist.Entries.Entry

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.Entry.float_fmt
documentation:
When a float of unknown precision is read, we do not want
to clip off significant digits, but neither do we want
to add too many decimal places. This function calculates
how many dps we need to keep not to lose precision when
handling ff params (by default, we clip to 8).
\:param flt: float, the number to be formatted
\:param fields: how many fields do we need overall in the fmt specifier
\:param dpmax: default limit on the number of decimal places
\:return: str, format specifier

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.Entry.__str__
documentation:
Fallback if no explicit formatting is implemented
\:return: str

A generic class representing a single line in the topology.
In an entry, the actual content and the comments are kept
in two separate variables.

classes.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryBonded
documentation:
This Entry subclass is intended for entries that correspond
to bonded interaction (bonds, pairs, angles, dihedrals)
between specific atoms in the topology

classes.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryParam

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryParam.match
documentation:
Checks if the entry matches an external atomtype list, given the interaction type
\:param ext_typelist: list of str, types to be checked
\:param int_type: str, integer identifier of the interaction type
\:return: bool, whether the entry matches the query

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryParam.__str__
documentation:
For cmaptypes, we rearrange lines to retrieve the matrix
format lost during read-in; for other entry types, we
delegate formatting to Subsection.fmt
:return:

This Entry subclass represents a line containing force field
parameters, e.g. bondtypes, angletypes, cmaptypes, pairtypes etc.
that map a set of atom types to a set of FF-specific values

classes.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryAtom

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryAtom.molname
documentation:
returns the name of the molecule this atom is part of
\:return: str, name of the molecule

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryAtom.ish
documentation:
Tells if an atom is a hydrogen (useful e.g. for hydrogen mass repartitioning)
WARNING not very relevant but might make mistakes with helium
\:param refstate: 'A' or 'B', alchemical state to take into account
\:return: bool, whether the atom is a hydrogen

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryAtom.sigma
documentation:
Returns the LJ sigma value of an atom (in nm)
\:return: float, the value of sigma

methods.name: gromologist.Entries.EntryAtom.epsilon
documentation:
Returns the LJ epsilon value of an atom (in kJ/mol)
\:return: float, the value of epsilon

This Entry subclass corresponds to atoms defined in
the [ atoms ] section of each molecule

functions.name: gromologist.DihOpt.mappable
documentation:
Wrapper for dual_annealing needed to make it parallelizable
through Pool.map() in DihOpt.optimize() (needs to be defined in the
global scope to be picklable)
\:param arg: tuple, combines system index, the DihOpt object, and the maxiter value
\:return: result of the dual_annealing fn

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.__init__
documentation:
The DihOpt is a dihedral optimizer that reads in (preferably) a Gaussian .log
file and a Gromacs topology with selected dihedral terms marked as DIHOPT in the comment field,
and then optimizes the dihedral parameters to minimize the RMSE between the QM and MM energies.
\:param top: a gml.Top object or str, Gromacs topology (either read-in or filename)
\:param qm_ref: str or list of str, path(s) to Gaussian .log files
\:param cutoff: float, will not attempt to fit data points above this threshold
\:param qm_unit: str, if qm_ref is a list of energies, this should be 'kcal/mol', 'kj/mol' or 'hartree'
\:param processes: int, how many independent fitting procedures should be launched (supports parallelism)
\:param tmpi: bool, whether to use Gromacs with thread-MPI in parallel execution
\:param kwargs: dict, if 'top' is a path, this will be passed to the gml.Top constructor

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.check_imports
documentation:
To keep the lib as lightweight as possible, we're not explicitly requiring scipy/numpy,
but to use DihOpt both are required, so here we look for them before running
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.calc_energy
documentation:
Calculates the energy profile for a given set of dihedral parameters
by running a rerun and extracting data from the resulting .xvg file
\:param ff_values: list of floats, dihedral parameters that will be set for the current iteration
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param cleanup: bool, whether to remove all files after the iteration
\:return: np.array, classical (MM) potential energies in kJ/mol for each frame in the trajectory

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.calc_rmse
documentation:
For a given set of dihedral parameters, runs the rerun and calculates the RMSE
between the QM data and the new MM energies
\:param ff_values: list of floats, dihedral parameters that will be set for the current iteration
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param cleanup: bool, whether to remove all files after the iteration
\:return: float, root-mean-square-error (in kJ/mol) between the QM and MM potential energy profiles

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.get_bounds
documentation:
Finds bounds for parameter optimization, odd ones are for angle offsets,
even ones for force constants
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:return: list of tuples, bounds for the optimization procedure

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.optimize
documentation:
Calculates initial values, then performs the actual optimization
using sklearn.optimize.dual_annealing either in a single process
(when self.processes is 1) or in parallel (when self.processes > 1)
\:param maxiter: int, at how many iterations to terminate
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.restart
documentation:
Can be used to reoptimize the parameters, using previous
best result as the new starting point
\:param maxiter: int, at how many iterations to terminate
\:return:

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.gmx_grompp
documentation:
Runs gmx grompp to prepare a .tpr file for the rerun
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param n: int, index of the topology
\:return:

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.gmx_mdrun
documentation:
Runs gmx mdrun with the '-rerun' option to extract MM energies
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param n: int, index of the topology
\:return:

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.gmx_energy
documentation:
\# TODO merge all
Runs gmx energy on the .edr file produced by the rerun, also
extracts energies (in kJ/mol) from it & shifts them to 0
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param n: int, index of the topology
\:return: numpy.array, energies of individual frames in kJ/mol

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.get_energy_term
documentation:
Checks the index of the 'Potential' term in gmx energy
(usually 8 or 9, depending on the presence of impropers)
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param n: int, index of the topology
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.cleanup
documentation:
Removes files on-the-fly to prevent errors at 99 backups
\:param sys: int, index of the system
\:param n: int, index of the topology
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.plot_fit
documentation:
If optimization is complete, plots the resulting energy profile against
both original MM energies and the QM reference
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.progress
documentation:
Constantly updates the curr_iter and lowest_iter_rmse attributes used for reporting
\:param \_: discarded
\:param f: float, current function value (RMSE)
\:param context: int, key corresponding to different outcomes in the optimization algorithm
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.write_mdp
documentation:
Writes an .mdp file with all options necessary for a rerun
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.gen_dirs
documentation:
Makes separate directories for all the subprocesses
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.find_gmx
documentation:
Attempts to find Gromacs executables to run gmx grompp/mdrun/energy
\:return: str, path to the gmx/gmx_mpi/gmx_d executable

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.read_gaussian_energies
documentation:
Function that parses a Gaussian .log file with a dihedral scan results,
extracting optimized geometries and reference QM energies; also saves
the trajectory/structure as .pdb files based on .top atom names
\:param log: str, name of the Gaussian .log file
\:param n_sys: int, index of the log file (in case there are many)
\:param traj: str, name of the output .pdb trajectory file
\:param structfile: str, name of the output .pdb structure file needed for gmx grompp
\:return: list, energies in kJ/mol

methods.name: gromologist.DihOpt.DihOpt.make_movie
documentation:
Uses Molywood to produce a movie showing the optimization results
along with the QM optimization trajectory, highlighting the dihedral
being optimized
\:return: None

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx._gmx_command
documentation:
Runs the specified gmx command, optionally passing keyworded or stdin arguments
\:param gmx_exe: str, a gmx executable
\:param command: str, the gmx command to launch
\:param answer: bool, whether to read & return the stderr + stdout of the command
\:param pass_values: iterable, optional values to pass to the command (like group selections in gmx trjconv)
\:param quiet: bool, whether to show gmx output
\:param fail_on_error: bool, whether to raise an error when the command crashes
\:param params: dict, for any "-key value" option to be included pass entry formatted as {"key": value}; to simply
pass a flag, value has to be True
\:return: str, stdout/stderr output from the command (if answer=True)

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.gen_mdp
documentation:
Produces a default .mdp file for the rerun
\:param fname: str, name of the output file
\:param runtype: str, "mini" for minimization or anything else for dynamics
\:param extra_args: dict, optional extra parameter: value pairs (will overwrite defaults); use \_\_ for -
\:return: None

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.find_gmx_dir
documentation:
Attempts to find Gromacs internal files to fall back to
when default .itp files are included using the
#include statement
\:param suppress: bool, whether to suppress messaging
\:param mpi: bool, whether to specifically look for gmx_mpi
\:return: tuple of str, path to share/gromacs/top directory and path to gmx executable

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.read_xvg
documentation:
Reads an .xvg file into a 2D list
\:param fname: str, .xvg file to read
\:param cols: list of int, columns to select
\:return: list of lists, numeric data from the .xvg file

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.get_legend
documentation:
Performs a dummy run of gmx energy to read the matching between terms and numbers
\:param gmx: str, path to the gmx executable
\:param fname: str, path to the .edr file
\:return: dict, matches between the terms' names and their consecutive numbers

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.get_residuetypes
documentation:
Gets residue names with their major types (Protein, DNA, RNA, Water, Ion)
\:return: dictionary of type_name: {set of residue names}

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.ndx
documentation:
Writes a .ndx file with groups g1, g2, ... defined by the
list of selections passed as input
\:param struct: gml.Pdb, a structure file
\:param selections: list of str, selections compatible with `struct`
\:param fname: str, name of the resulting .ndx file (default is 'gml.ndx'
\:param append: str, if provided then the groups will be appended to an existing file
\:param group_names: list of str, groups will be named with these names
\:return: list of str, names of the group

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.frames_count
documentation:
Runs gmx check to calculate the number of frames in a specified trajectory
\:param trajfile: str, path to/name of the trajectory
\:param gmx: str, optionally the name of the Gromacs executable
\:return: int, number of frames found

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.calc_gmx_energy
documentation:
Calculates selected energy terms given a structure/topology pair or structure/topology/trajectory set.
\:param struct: str, path to the structure file
\:param topfile: str, path to the topology file
\:param gmx: str, path to the gmx executable (if not found in the \$PATH)
\:param quiet: bool, whether to print gmx output to the screen
\:param traj: str, path to the trajectory (optional)
\:param terms: str or list, terms which will be calculated according to gmx energy naming (can also be "all")
\:param cleanup: bool, whether to remove intermediate files (useful for debugging)
\:param group_a: str, selection defining group A to calculate interactions between group A and B (or only energetics of group A if group_b is left unspecified)
\:param group_b: str, selection defining group B to calculate interactions between group A and B
\:param sum_output: bool, whether to add a term "sum" that will contain all terms added up
\:param kwargs: dict, options that will be added to the .mdp file
\:return: dict of lists, one list of per-frame values per each selected term

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.calc_gmx_dhdl
documentation:
Calculates selected energy terms given a structure/topology pair or structure/topology/trajectory set.
\:param struct: str, path to the structure file
\:param topfile: str, path to the topology file
\:param gmx: str, path to the gmx executable (if not found in the \$PATH)
\:param quiet: bool, whether to print gmx output to the screen
\:param traj: str, path to the trajectory (optional)
\:param cleanup: bool, whether to remove intermediate files (useful for debugging)
\:param kwargs: dict, additional "-key value" parameter sets to be passed to mdrun
\:param abs_path: str, absolute path for all temporarily created files
\:return: dict of lists, one list of per-frame values per each selected term

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.compare_topologies_by_energy
documentation:
Given two topologies and a structure file, checks if both yield
the same potential energy
\:param struct: str, path to the reference structure file
\:param topfile1: str, path to the first Top file
\:param topfile2: str, path to the other Top file
\:param gmx: str, optional path to the gmx executable if different than simply 'gmx'
\:param quiet: bool, optional to silence gmx output
\:param group_a: str, 1st selection for which pairwise interactions will be calculated, or selection for the subset that should be compared
\:param group_b: str, 2nd selection for which pairwise interactions will be calculated
\:return: bool, whether the energies are identical

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.prepare_system
documentation:
Implements a full system preparation workflow (parsing the structure with pdb2gmx,
setting the box size, adding solvent and ions, minimizing, generating a final
merged topology + structure with added chains)
\:param struct: str, path to the structure file
\:param ff: str, name of the force field to be chosen (by default interactive)
\:param water: str, name of the water model to be used
\:param box: str, box type (default is dodecahedron)
\:param cation: str, the cation to be used (default is K)
\:param anion: str, the anion to be used (default is CL)
\:param ncation: int, the number of cations to be used (default is from concentration)
\:param nanion: int, the number of anions to be used (default is from concentration)
\:param ion_conc: float, the ion concentration to be applied (default 0.15)
\:param resize_box: bool, whether to generate a new box size based on the -d option of gmx editconf
\:param box_margin: float, box margin passed to editconf to set box size (default is 1.5 nm)
\:param explicit_box_size: list, the a/b/c lengths of the box vector (in nm); this overrides box_margin
\:param quiet: bool, whether to print gmx output to the screen
\:param topology: str, if passed gmx pdb2gmx will be skipped
\:param minimize: bool, whether to do energy minimization at the end
\:param maxwarn: int, how many warnings to tolerate in grompp
\:param solvent_from: str, reproduce solvent data from a chosen topology file
\:return: None

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.get_groups
documentation:
Extracts a dictionary with group definitions for a given structure,
optionally augmented with information from an existing .ndx file
\:param fname: str, the structure file (.gro/.pdb/.tpr/...)
\:param ndx: str, the optional index file
\:return: dict, matches from group names to group indices

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.obj_or_str
documentation:
Makes sure we can always use either the string (path to file) or the gml.Pdb/gml.Top object
and internally we will always handle the desired object anyway (either path or gml object)
\:param pdb: str (path) or gml.Pdb object
\:param top: str (path) or gml.Top object
\:param return_path: bool, whether we should return the path (if True) or a gml obj (if False)
\:return: str or gml.Pdb or gml.Top

functions.name: gromologist.Gmx.process_trajectories
documentation:
A one-step trajectory processor that tries to fix PBC issues, allows to quickly
remove solvent and stride the trajectory, as well as merge multiple simulation parts into one
\:param mask: str, a regular expression matching the trajectories to be processed (like "run.part00\*xtc")
\:param tpr: str, a matching .tpr file (can be PDB if pbc is not 'cluster' or 'mol')
\:param group_cluster: str, name of the group that will be used for clustering (if pbc = 'cluster')
\:param group_output: str, name of the group that will be used for output
\:param pbc: str, PBC treatmemt that will be passed to trjconv
\:param stride: int, keep every n-th frame in the resulting .xtc
\:param ndx: str, optional .ndx file to define groups
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.__init__
documentation:
A pool of workers that will initialize all simulations
and collect the results afterwards
\:param struct: str, a valid .gro or .pdb structure file for the system
\:param top: str, a .top topology file for the system
\:param xtc0: str, a trajectory capturing the equilibrium ensemble for state A (can also be .dcd or any other
format accepted by MDTraj)
\:param xtc1: str, a trajectory capturing the equilibrium ensemble for state B
\:param nruns: int, number of A->B runs (overall, twice this many runs will be performed)
\:param length: int, length of a single trajectory (in ps)
\:param gmx: str, Gromacs executable (gmx by default)
\:param maxwarn: int, max number of warnings accepted by grompp
\:param convergence: bool, whether to do convergence analysis
\:param alias: str, a base name for all output files
\:param debug: bool, whether to print detailed convergence info
\:param offset: int, start numbering workers from this number (useful when more jobs are added post-factum)
\:param kwargs: dict of str:str pairs, will be converted into additional .mdp options

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.run
documentation:
The main routine: extracts initial frames,
runs grompp and mdrun in parallel
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.analyze
documentation:
The analysis routine: performed after the run,
reads all data files, processes them and
prints out the results and/or convergence data
:return:

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.bhattacharyya
documentation:
Calculates the Bhattacharyya (similarity) coefficient between distributions d0 and d1
\:param d0: np.array, rho_0, density of A->B work values
\:param d1: np.array, rho_1, density of B->A -work values
\:param grid: np.array, the corresponding X values
\:return: np.array of shape [1,1]

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.analyze_convergence
documentation:
Performs a series of bootstraps for a number of
subsample sizes, then plots the resulting means/
standard deviations to a file
\:param w0: np.array, work values for A->B
\:param w1: np.array, -work values for A->B
\:param grid: np.array, the X values, as in the original plot
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.boot
documentation:
Performs a single bootstrap resampling,
parallelized through ProcessPoolExecutor
\:param params: a 5-element tuple of input parameters:
A->B work, B->A -work, number of subsamples to draw, original X values, random seed
\:return: float, the KDE solution for the intersection

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.solve_kde
documentation:
Finds the intersection point based
on KDE densities
\:param d0: np.array,
\:param d1: np.array,
\:param grid: np.array,
\:return: float, abscissa of intersection

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.solve_cgi
documentation:
Finds the intersection point based on the CGI
estimator (intersection of two Gaussians)
\:param w0: np.array, work values for A->B
\:param w1: np.array, -work values for B->A
\:return: float, abscissa of intersection

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.get_opt_kde
documentation:
Performs KDE (kernel density estimation) with a Gaussian
kernel, and optimizes the bandwidth using the LeaveOneOut
cross-validation if requested
\:param y: np.array, work values
\:param x: np.array, X values for the density
\:param bandwidth: if None, optimize; if a float, use this value
\:return: np.array, the estimated density

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.plot_results
documentation:
Plots the two densities, their intersection
and all individual work values at the bottom;
also writes the precise solution to a file
(this is admittedly a bit confusing)
\:param w0: np.array, work values for A->B
\:param w1: np.array, -work values for B->A
\:param p0: np.array, X:Y two columns for the first density
\:param p1: np.array, X:Y two columns for the second density
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.drop_frames
documentation:
Selects frames from the provided equilibrium trajectories, either in equal intervals or randomly
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.mdrun_multi
documentation:
Runs gmx mdrun with the -multidir option for maximum efficiency
\:param runtype: str, 'mini', 'eq' or 'dyn'
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.CrooksPool.ncpus
documentation: Returns the total number of available CPUs across all nodes.

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.Crooks.__init__
documentation:
A single worker instance
\:param master: a CrooksPool instance
\:param num: ID of the worker
\:param initlambda: initial alchemical lambda (0 or 1)

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.Crooks.mkdir
documentation:
Attempts to create a directory if not there yet
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.Crooks.prep_runs
documentation:
Prepares the run by writing customized .mdp files
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.Crooks.grompp_me
documentation:
Checks for the .tpr file, runs grompp if not present
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Crooks.Crooks.analyze_me
documentation:
Reads the .xvg file, removes potential duplicates (if the
job was restarted), and integrates dH/dl to calculate work
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.__init__
documentation:
Here we want to have:
: - a unique representation of the section (header/ID)
  - a list of entries
  - a binding to the Top class that holds the Section object

```
param content

list of strings, entire content of the section

param section

a Section instance that contains this Subsection
```

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.yield_entry
documentation:
Decides which Entry subclass to return
based on which Subsection subclass evokes this fn
\:param line: str, a line to be converted into an Entry instance
\:return: Entry, an instance of the proper Entry subclass

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.__str__
documentation:
As section headers can be repeated, each section is denoted
by a header and ID (ID corresponding to the consecutive numbering
of the specific header, e.g. bonds-3 is the third "bonds" section
\:return: str, section label

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.__iter__
documentation:
Useful if we want to iterate over entries as "for entry in subsection",
allows us to mark self.\_entries as private
\:return: self

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.add_entry
documentation:
Adds a single entry to the subsection, either at the end
or in a specified position
\:param new_entry: gml.Entry, entry to be added
\:param position: where to add the entry (None is at the end)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.add_entries
documentation:
Adds multiple entries to the subsection, either at the end
or in a specified position
\:param new_entries_list: list of gml.Entry, entries to be added
\:param position: where to add the entries (None is at the end)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.set_entry
documentation:
Sets content of a specified entry
\:param line_number: int, which entry to modify
\:param new_line: str, new content of the entry
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.Subsection.get_entry
documentation:
Returns entry specified by line number
\:param line_number: int, which entry to return
\:return: gml.Entry, subsection entry

classes.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.entries_bonded
documentation:
Lists all entries that are of EntryBonded type (i.e. excluding comments,
headers, empty lines etc)
\:return: list of EntryBonded instances

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.sort
documentation:
In case we want to sort entries after some are added at the end of the section
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.change_interaction_type
documentation:
Changes interaction type of the whole section
\:param change_from: str/int, original interaction type
\:param change_to: str/int, target interaction type
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded._sorting_fn
documentation:
Comments should go first, then we sort based on first, second,
... column of the section
\:param entry: Entry, entry to be sorted
\:return: int, ordering number

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.explicit_defines
documentation:
Substitutes predefined fields with their corresponding values
as specified by the #DEFINE preprocessor commands
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.add_ff_params
documentation:
Adds explicit values of FF parameters in sections 'bonds', 'angles', 'dihedrals'
\:param force_all: bool, whether to add params from scratch if some are assigned already
\:param external_paramsB: gml.SectionParam, if reading parameters from an external file for state B
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.find_used_ff_params
documentation:
Looks up a list of FF parameters that are actually used in the molecular
system at hand
\:return: list of str, labels of used parameters

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded._find_used_ff_params
documentation:
Identifies the FF parameter(s) that is used for the particular
bonded interaction entry
\:param entry: an EntryBonded instance, an entry we're finding the parameter for
\:param subsect_params: a list of SubsectionParam instances containing candidate parameters for the entry
\:return: list of str, labels of used parameters

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded.find_missing_ff_params
documentation:
Identifies FF parameters that cannot by matched to the existing set of
bonded interactions, allowing to find & target these for fixing
\:param fix_by_analogy: dict, tells how to choose new parameters from existing equivalent types
\:param fix_B_from_A: bool, chooses missing parameters assuming B-state should have similar types to A-state
\:param fix_A_from_B: bool, chooses missing parameters assuming A-state should have similar types to B-state
\:param fix_dummy: bool, adds dummy (all-0) parameters
\:param once: bool, only shows the missing parameter once if repeated
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded._add_ff_params_to_entry
documentation:
Given a bonded term (e.g. "21 24 26 5") converts it to atomtypes,
finds the respective FF parameters and adds them to the bonded entry
\:param entry: Entry, an EntryBonded instance to add FF params to
\:param subsect_params: list, SubsectionParam instances that hold all FF params
\:param subsect_params_B: list, a separate SubsectionParam instances for state B if needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionBonded._check_parm_type
documentation:
Finds number codes for interaction type, e.g. CHARMM uses angletype '5' (urey-bradley)
while Amber uses angletype '1' (simple harmonic); this will usually be one number
but if subsections are merged, we can have mixed interaction types
\:return: list of strs, interaction types

SubsectionBonded contains a subsection with entries corresponding to bonded terms,
e.g., bonds or dihedrals; should be included in SectionMol

classes.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam.__add__
documentation:
Added for the purpose of merging subsections with
identical headers
\:param other: other SubsectionParam instance
\:return: a new SubsectionParam instance resulting from the merger

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam.sort
documentation:
In case we want to sort entries after some are added at the end of the section
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam._combine_entries
documentation:
When merging parameter sets, makes sure there are no duplications, and in these cases resolves conflicts
\:param other_subsection: the gml.Subsection instance from which entries will be added to merge files
\:param overwrite: bool, whether to accept new parameters in case of a mismatch
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam._sorting_fn
documentation:
Comments should go first, then we sort based on first, second,
... column of the section
\:param entry: Entry, entry to be sorted
\:return: int, ordering number

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam._check_parm_type
gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionParam._process_cmap
documentation:
Reads a multiline entry from the [ cmaptypes ] section
and converts it into an array that can be later properly printed
\:return: None

SubsectionParam contains force field parameters;
should be included in SectionParam

classes.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionAtom

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionAtom.get_dicts
documentation:
dicts are not always needed and are costly to calculate,
so only fill in the values when explicitly asked to
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionAtom._mol_type_nums
documentation:
Provides bindings between atomnumber and atomtype
and vice versa for each molecule identified in
the topology
\:return: tuple of dicts, each dict contains molname:(type:num) and
molname:(num:type) bindings

SubsectionAtom contains definitions of all atoms in the molecule;
should be contained in SectionMol

classes.name: gromologist.Subsection.SubsectionHeader
documentation:
SubsectionHeader contains the [ moleculetype ] section;
should be contained in SectionMol

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.atoms_to_add
documentation:
For each atom group, identifies a set of atoms, bonds, anchors etc.
that have to be included when implementing the mutation
\:return: tuple of lists, the required changes

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.atoms_to_remove
documentation:
For each atom group, identifies a set of atoms
that have to be removed when implementing the mutation
:return:

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.check_chiral
documentation:
Makes sure the correct HA is removed in case of glycine
\:param struct: gml.Pdb instance
\:param ca: gml.Atom instance
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.groups
documentation:
Defines atoms constituting each atomic group
(akin e.g. to a methyl or methylene group) in side chains
\:param key: str, name of the group
\:return: list of atoms in the group

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.afters
documentation:
For each atomic group, identifies by name atoms immediately
preceding the ones to be introduced (allowing for variation)
\:param key: str, name of the group
\:return: list of atoms preceding the corresponding atom in self.groups[key] (or tuples if multiple names
are accepted)

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.bonds
documentation:
For each atomic group, identifies chemical bonds that connect atoms
of that group, as well as the group to the previous one
\:param key: str, name of the group
\:return: list of lists, each containing a pair of atoms to be bonded (or tuples for ambiguous names)

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.anchors
documentation:
For each atomic group, identifies sequences of atoms that define
the geometry of the newly built group (through routines defined
in gml.Pdb.\_vector)
\:param key: str, name of the group
\:return: list of lists

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.ring_closing_bonds
documentation:
If a residue contains a ring, this fn defines an extra bond
that closes the ring (might require special treatment)
\:param key: str, name of the group
\:return: list of tuples, each defines a ring-closing bond

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.aminoacids
documentation:
Lists groups that make up individual amino acids
\:param key: str, 1-letter code of the amino acid
\:return: list, groups that define the side chain

methods.name: gromologist.Mutant.ProteinMutant.bond_lengths
documentation:
Defines bond lengths of pairs of atoms
\:param bonded_pair: list of str, names of the elements that create the bond
\:return: float, length of the bond

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.Mod.__init__
documentation:
Base class to work with parameter modifications.
The class is actually used for the calculation of original
(unmodified) quantities, and is subclassed by ModParam,
ModNbfix and ModAtom.
\:param top: list of Top objects corresponding to the modified topology
\:param structure: str, path to a structure file (.gro or .pdb) compatible with the topology
\:param selections: list of str, selections that will define atoms to be modified

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.Mod.__str__
documentation:
string representation of a Mod object, should be unique
\:return: mod one-letter code + Mod index, 1-based

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.Mod.__eq__
documentation:
assumes unique numbering of instances
\:param other: another Mod (or subclass) instance to compare with
\:return: bool

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.Mod.save_mod
documentation:
wraps Top's save_mod to catch the file name
\:param path: str, filename to save .top to
\:param name: str, name of the file
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.Mod.goto_mydir
documentation:
shortcut to the ModAtom's working directory
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModAtom.__init__
documentation:
Subclass to work with modified atomic params (that is,
sigma and epsilon defined for individual types, or charge defined
for individual atoms).
The class takes in a set of atoms that all correspond
to a single atom type (has to be the same for all atoms);
these atoms' type will be cloned from "type" to "Ytype",
and the corresponding sigma/epsilon/charge parameters will be modified.
\:param top: a Top object corresponding to the modified topology
\:param selections: list of str, selections that will define atoms to be modified
\:param changes: single character denoting the change to be performed

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModAtom.__repr__
documentation:
string representation of a Mod object, should be unique
\:return: mod one-letter code + Mod index, 1-based

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModAtom.mod_chg
documentation:
looks for a line in topology section 'atoms'
and modifies it as needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModAtom.mod_sigma_eps
documentation:
looks for a line in topology section 'atomtypes'
and modifies it as needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModAtom.types_are_consistent
documentation:
if many atoms are passed in a single input line, need to make sure
they are all of the same type
\:return: True if all are of the same type, False otherwise

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModNbfix.__init__
documentation:
Subclass to work with modified NBFIX params (pairwise corrections,
sigma and epsilon defined for pairs of types).
The class allows two lists (actually, tuples) of atoms that correspond
to two atom types (atomtypes have to be consistent within each tuple);
these atoms' types will be cloned from (type1, type2) to (Xtype1,
Ytype2), and the corresponding NBFIX parameters will be modified.
The modification can be applied to all atoms bearing the specific type
as well as only a subgroup of these.
\:param top: a Top object corresponding to the modified topology
\:param selections: list of str, selections that will define atoms to be modified
\:param changes: single character denoting the change to be performed

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModNbfix.__repr__
documentation:
string representation of a Mod object, should be unique
\:return: mod one-letter code + Mod index, 1-based

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModNbfix.mod_nb_sigma_eps
documentation:
looks for a line in topology section 'nonbond_params'
and modifies it as needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModNbfix.prefix_type
documentation:
looks for a line in topology section 'atoms'
and modifies it as needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModNbfix.types_are_consistent
documentation:
if many atoms are passed in a single input line, need to make sure
they are all of the same type; raises ValueError if this is not the case
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModParam.__init__
documentation:
Subclass to work with modified bonded params (angles and dihedrals).
The class allows a lists of tuples of atoms that each corresponds
to a single bonded term, angle or dihedral (all need to have
the same sets of atomtypes for the calculation to be meaningful);
the corresponding parameters will be modified in the molecule definition.
The modification can be applied to all atoms bearing the specific type
as well as only a subgroup of these.
\:param top: Top, a Top object corresponding to the modified topology
\:param selections: list of str, selections that will define atoms to be modified
\:param changes: str, single character denoting the change to be performed

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModParam.__repr__
documentation:
string representation of a Mod object, should be unique
\:return: mod one-letter code + Mod index, 1-based

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModParam.mod_ang
documentation:
looks for lines in topology section 'angles'
and modifies them as needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModParam.mod_dih
documentation:
looks for lines in topology section 'dihedrals'
and modifies them as needed
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ModParam.types_are_consistent
documentation:
checks whether all input atoms share the same type signature,
(t1 t2 t3 t4) for dihedrals or (t1 t2 t3) for angles;
raises ValueError if not the case
\:return: None

classes.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_mod
documentation:
Adds a new modified topology for derivative calculation
\:param top: str or gml.Top, the topology compatible with the system
\:param structure: str, a path to the Pdb or Gro file
\:param modtype: str, type of the modification, one of the following: c, s, e, n, m, a, d
\:param selections: list of str, selections that will define atoms to be modified
\:param period: int, periodicity for a dihedral modification (if applicable)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_all_nbfix_mods
documentation:
Automatically adds all NBFIX modifications that can be conceived from the parameter set
\:param top: str or gml.Top, the topology compatible with the system
\:param structure: str, a path to the Pdb or Gro file
\:param typelist: optional, only consider the types listed in constructing NBFIXes
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_all_dihedral_mods
documentation:
Automatically adds all dihedral modifications that can be conceived from the parameter set
(one entry at a time, e.g. CA-CT-CT-OH with periodicity 1 produces one modification);
can be restricted to dihedrals involving selected atom types
\:param top: str or gml.Top, the topology compatible with the system
\:param structure: str, a path to the Pdb or Gro file
\:param molecules: optional list of str, only add dihedrals from these molecules (default is whole system)
\:param selector_type: optional, only consider dihedrals that have this type (if str)
or any of these types (if list)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_all_charge_mods
documentation:
Automatically adds all dihedral modifications that can be conceived from the parameter set
(one entry at a time, e.g. CA-CT-CT-OH with periodicity 1 produces one modification);
can be restricted to dihedrals involving selected atom types
\:param top: str or gml.Top, the topology compatible with the system
\:param structure: str, a path to the Pdb or Gro file
\:param molecules: optional list of str, only add dihedrals from these molecules (default is whole system)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff._add_lj_mods
documentation:
Automatically adds calculations of derivatives with respect to
all sigma or epsilon values in the system at hand
\:param top: either str (filename) or gml.Top instance
\:param structure: str, filename of the .pdb or .gro corresponding to the .top file
\:param which: str, 'sigma' or 'epsilon'
\:param exclude: list, optional selection of types to exclude from the calculation
\:param molecules: list or str, optional list of molecules from which the types will be selected
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_traj
documentation:
Adds a trajectory on which the derivatives will be calculated,
including pre-calculated data and frame-wise weights
\:param top: str or gml.Top, the topology compatible with the trajectory
\:param traj: str, path to the trajectory
\:param datasets: dict, named datasets in the format alias: list of [float/int/str]
\:param weights: list of float, per-frame weights (e.g. from Umbrella Sampling, Metadynamics or other reweighing
schemes)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.get_traj
documentation:
Gets a trajectory by its integer ID
\:param trajid: int, ID of the trajectory
\:return: dict, the trajectory and its associated data

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_data_to_traj
documentation:
If not specified in self.add_traj, data can be added to the trajectory
in a separate step
\:param trajid: int, ID of the trajectory
\:param datasets: dict, named datasets in the format alias: list of [float/int/str]
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.add_weights_to_traj
documentation:
If not specified in self.add_traj, weights can be added to the trajectory
in a separate step
\:param trajid: int, ID of the trajectory
\:param weights: list of float, per-frame weights (e.g. from Umbrella Sampling, Metadynamics or other reweighing
schemes)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.equal_tops
documentation:
Checks (in a quick and approximate way) if two topologies are identical
\:param top1: gml.Top, first top to check
\:param top2: gml.Top, second top to check
\:return: bool, whether top1 and top2 are identical

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.prep_files
documentation:
Prepares the directory tree for the calculations
:return:

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.launch_reruns
documentation:
Launches reruns for each legal mod-trajectory combination
\:param parallel: bool, whether to use parallel calculations
\:param maxcores: int, optional number of processes
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.launch_rerun
documentation:
Launches an individual alchemical rerun and reads the data, can be parallelized
(skips the calculation if already performed)
\:param mod_traj_ds: tuple, contains the mod, traj and dataset objects
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.calc_weights
documentation:
Normalizes weights (or adds constant weights if not specified)
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.run
documentation:
Runs the set of calculations and prepares everything
for data extraction
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.get_mod
documentation:
Gets the desired gml.Mod subclass instance by counter (ID)
\:param counter: int, ID of the modified topology
\:return: gml.Mod, selected modified topology

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.get_length
documentation:
Gets the length of the trajectory from the length
of the derivatives file (provided reruns have been performed)
\:param trajid: int, ID of the trajectory
\:return: int, length of the trajectory

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.dataset_is_numeric
documentation:
Checks if the user-specified dataset is numeric or string-based
(in the latter case discrete states will be used)
\:param dataset: str, alias of the dataset
\:return: bool, whether the dataset is numeric

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.get_flat_data
documentation:
Selects all numeric datasets that correspond to a given alias
and provides flat (1-D) lists for data, weights, and derivatives
\:param binning_dataset: str, alias of the dataset used for binning/thresholding
\:param deriv_dataset: str, alias of the dataset used for derivative calculation (can be same as binning_dataset)
\:param mod: gml.Mod
\:return: tuple of lists: data, weights, and derivatives

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.calc_discrete_derivatives
documentation:
Calculates selected derivatives for a number of discrete states
\:param dataset: str, alias for the dataset
\:param free_energy: bool, whether the derivative should be of free energy or of observable/CV
\:param threshold: list, for continues dataset specifies 2N boundaries used to define N discrete states
\:param cv_dataset: str, if specified then derivatives will be calculated based on 'dataset' but binning will be based on this
\:param noe: bool, if free_energy is False, then this switches on calculations based on averaging the -6th power (use one state)
\:param bootstrap: bool, whether to estimate uncertainty using bootstrap
performed based on 'cv_dataset'
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.calc_profile_derivatives
documentation:
Calculates selected derivatives of a profile
\:param dataset: str, alias for the dataset
\:param free_energy: bool, whether the derivative should be of free energy or of observable/CV
\:param nbins: int, number of bins that will be used to calculate the profile
\:param cv_dataset: str, if specified then derivatives will be calculated based on 'dataset' but binning will be
performed based on 'cv_dataset'
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.write_discrete_derivatives
documentation:
Writes the derivatives to individual files in each working subdirectory
\:param dataset:
\:param free_energy:
\:param bootstrap:
\:return:

methods.name: gromologist.ThDiff.ThermoDiff.print_discrete_derivatives
documentation:
Prints discrete derivatives to the screen or a file
\:param dataset: str, for which dataset the derivatives should be selected
\:param free_energy: bool, whether the 1st state should be set to reference (equal to 0.0)
\:param outfile: str, optional filename if the result is to be printed to a file
\:param bootstrap: bool, whether to add uncertainty
\:param target: list or float, if specified, will also calculate required change so that req_change = (target-mean)/derivative
\:return: None

PLEASE NOTE: certain box types are not suitable for reruns in Gromacs
(e.g. Amber periodic octahedron). If that's your case, make sure your
molecules are whole, and use an external tool (e.g. MDTraj) to edit
your box angles and box side lengths to ensure sufficient space between
periodic images (PME is not used in reruns anyway, just plain cut-off).

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.__init__
documentation:
A class to represent and contain the Gromacs topology file and provide

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.from_selection

Returns a new .top file corresponding to the specified selection

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.save_from_selection
documentation:
Generates and saves a new .top file corresponding to the specified selection
\:param selection: str, a Gromologist-compatible selection
\:param filename: str, name of the resulting topology file
\:return: None

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.system
documentation:
Returns a list of tuples with the contents of the system,
following the [ molecules ] section of a topology

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.print

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top._from_text

A simple wrapper to generate a topology from an in-memory string object

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.molecules

A property attribute returning the list of all SectionMols corresponding to molecules

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.alchemical_molecules

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.parameters

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.atoms

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.defaults
documentation:
Returns a dictionary with the values set in the [ defaults ] section

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.remove_molecule

Removes a molecule definition and the corresponding entry from the system

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.merge_molecules
documentation:
Combines two molecules into a single [ moleculetype ]

\:param molname2: str or int, name of molecule 2 or index of molecule 2

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.explicit_multiple_molecules

If a molecule has multiple copies, this transforms it to an explicit

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.select_atoms

methods.name: gromologist.Topology.Top.select_atom
documentation:
